pattern: Mapping the target to +1 and everything else to -1 turns the majority test into a positive-sum test, so the answer counts prefix-sum pairs that strictly increase.
hints: The target is the strict majority of a subarray exactly when, scoring +1 for target and -1 otherwise, the subarray's score is positive.
With a prefix sum P, the subarray [l, r] scores P[r+1] - P[l], so you need to count index pairs i < j with P[j] > P[i].
Walk the prefix sums and, for each new value, count how many earlier prefix sums were strictly smaller; sum those counts.

edge_inputs.description: five subarrays where 2 is the majority
exactly half is not a strict majority
target appears but never as majority of any window

stress.description: random small-alphabet array so the target is the majority in many windows
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(1000, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 4) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 4)
    return [nums, target]
stress.note: quadratic reference comfortably fits this size
